hard_gates.requirement: Binds the declared thread as manager, names goal/success/stop/boundaries, and refreshes a bounded checkpoint before worker creation because unresolved forward state exists.

Routes durable lanes to titled worker threads, supplies complete delegation packets including scope ownership, subagent-first policy or named exception, result shape, claim ceiling, and stop conditions.
Uses a fresh non-fork, non-rollover verifier or judge for acceptance and does not treat implementer self-check as independent review.
Does not claim actual thread creation, execution, validation success, registration, default-route safety, production readiness, or owner approval without evidence.
Contains no raw/private/secret material, runtime absolute path, unbounded fanout, or overlap-unsafe write routing.
Contains no golden output, golden-derived wording requirement, prior candidate content, or fabricated command/file-read claim.

dimensions.full_credit: Clear goal binding and justified manager/worker/verifier topology with lifecycle decision.
Every worker/verifier packet is self-contained and includes all contract-minimum fields and write ownership.
Checkpoint content is compact, durable, conditional, and excludes transcripts/payloads.
Plans status inspection, non-destructive integration, deterministic validators, workflow-check, and evidence-sensitive claims.
Preserves public/private/secret, independence, recursion, registration, and default-route boundaries.
Packets and next actions are directly actionable and easy for a manager to route.

evaluation_instructions.score_rule: A hard-gate failure forces final_quality=fail regardless of numeric score.
evaluation_instructions.wording_rule: Judge semantic contract satisfaction; do not require any particular phrasing or layout.

no_golden_attestation: Derived only from the current workflow.yaml and step_graph.yaml; no golden or candidate body was read or used.
